title: Snake Robot — Serpentine Locomotion Kinematics
content: A snake robot uses serpenoid curve gait: joint angle θ_i(t) = A·sin(2πt/T + i·φ) + offset. Forward thrust arises from anisotropic friction (lateral > axial) between links and ground. Minimum 6 servo joints achieve locomotion.
extended_content: Gaits: lateral undulation (most common, 5–9 joints), rectilinear (concertina), sidewinding (sand/slope). Curvature κ(s) = A·k·cos(ks + φ) follows Hirose's serpenoid curve. Lateral/axial friction ratio μ_lat/μ_ax ≥ 2 required. Joint spacing L (link length) controls propulsion: v_snake ≈ 0.3–0.5 × (A·ω·L) for 8-link robot. Servo torque per joint: T = m_distal × g × L/2 (static hold). Android Bluetooth HC-05 / BLE app sends (A, T, φ) parameters to Arduino. Each servo driven by 9–12 V supply; PWM 50 Hz, 1–2 ms pulse width.
units: θ: rad, A: rad (amplitude), T: s (period), φ: rad (phase shift)
source: Hirose, Biologically Inspired Robots, 1993; Transeth et al., IEEE Trans. Robotics 2008; Liljeback, Snake Robot Locomotion, Springer 2012
